Moved as the manuals noted: now threeway/gate.py:158 (was cited gate.py:95). `$ grep -rn 'run_gate' --include='*.py' .` -> every non-def hit is under tests/unit/ (test_threeway_gate*.py, test_threeway_slice2_adversarial.py) or internal comments in predicate.py/reducer.py/envelope.py/gate.py. `$ grep -rn 'RefEventStore|run_gate|verify_and_reduce|from threeway' --include='*.py' .` filtered to non-test/non-pkg -> ZERO hits (no web_server.py, no daemon, no cron, no scripts/ caller). run_gate IS production-quality + TOTAL (gate.py:158-234: never raises after CAS, well_formed guard, ADR-039/040/041); it just has nothing invoking it on the live bus.
steps.next_action: Write a merge-gate daemon/runner (or CLI) that constructs RefEventStore(repo), calls run_gate(candidate_id, store, repo, registry_dir=coordination/threeway/keys, bus_id='prod', main_ref, gate_seat='merge-gate') on a trigger (e.g. release_requested), and executes the merge on a clean GateResult. Requires the merge-gate.pub in the registry + the live bus.

steps.step: Slice 3 strategic loop + T2/T3 co_sign_satisfied (does it return False for T2/T3 today?)
steps.evidence: co_sign_satisfied does NOT return False for T2/T3 unconditionally — it is fully implemented and fail-closed. threeway/tier.py:39-61: T0/T1 -> True; T2 -> requires cross-provider independence + _t2_mirror_cosign (mirror pair's verifier GO bound to integration_sha, tier.py:90-98); T3 -> adds _t3_cross_provider_re_verify with overseer nonce freshness (ADR-043, tier.py:101-138) + _two_distinct_human_approvals on key-bound roster seats (ADR-043, tier.py:141-170). Live probe `$ python -c '...co_sign_satisfied...'` -> T0:True, T1:True, T2(no facts):False, T3(no facts):False, T2(same-provider):False — i.e. it returns True ONLY when the required signed facts exist, fail-closed otherwise. Identity from overseer-signed assignments + key-bound seat token, never the unsigned signer tail (tier.py:32-36). Confirmed wired into the gate path (run_gate -> verify_and_reduce -> predicate.py:126 -> co_sign_satisfied) and exercised E2E: test_run_gate_completes_full_t3_through_signed_gate passes (within the 329-passed threeway suite). PARTIAL because the tactical event builders (threeway/loop.py build_candidate_events) are a library for tests/demo with no live driver, and the scope-(b) STRATEGIC loop (overseer emitter that issues fresh re_verify_challenge nonces + approver_roster, and same-pair assignment supersede — ARCHITECTURE.md:1816-1818,1820-1834) is not built as a running process; ADR-043 closed the T3 freshness/per-approver GATE LOGIC but the nonce-EMITTER discipline rests on the overseer (tier.py:112-118).
steps.next_action: No code fix needed for the gate predicate (built + green). To go live: build the scope-(b) overseer/strategic emitter that issues fresh per-cycle re_verify_challenge nonces + approver_roster facts and handles same-pair assignment supersession, and provision each chief approver's <chief>.pub (else T3 human_approvals drop as unknown-seat -> T3 stays PENDING, ARCHITECTURE.md:1830-1833).

flip_preconditions: Key provisioning DONE: run `python -m threeway.keys_bootstrap` on a trusted non-candidate-executing host; commit all 9 <seat>.pub to coordination/threeway/keys/ (currently 0 committed — README.md only); distribute private .ed25519 keys to off-repo keystores. Hard blocker — without it every load-bearing event drops as unknown-seat. (threeway/keys_bootstrap.py:13; verified `git ls-files | grep -c .pub` -> 0)
Chief/overseer approver keys provisioned: each T3 human approver needs its own <chief>.pub in the registry or its approval is dropped and T3 stays PENDING (ARCHITECTURE.md:1830-1833).
Freeze the coordination tree for the flip window — no concurrent refs/threeway/* writer (ADR-044 op precondition, DECISIONS.md:2302).
Clean `divergence.diverge` check: projected event set == live sent count (threeway/divergence.py:40; ARCHITECTURE.md:1786-1788).
Successful force=True DRY-RUN of run_cutover asserting ready_to_flip (DECISIONS.md:2303).
Out-of-band pre-run ref snapshot captured (belt-and-suspenders beyond cutover's internal _snapshot_refs; DECISIONS.md:2303-2304).

Explicit USER confirmation of the live flip — DECISIONS.md:2306 + 2337: 'The live flip remains GATED on explicit user confirmation' / 'remains gated on explicit user confirmation with the ADR-044 operational preconditions unchanged.'
Expect a ~50-min O(n^2) append loop over ~768 events with NO progress output during the real run — do not abort as a hang (cutover.py:108-110).
activation.notes:

contradictions.which_is_right: Truth-sources + sibling UNIFIED doc win. The correct citation is "spec §8 item 8, line 400". Verified via `grep -n '## 8\|item 8' ...2026-06-19...design.md` and `sed -n '400p'`.
contradictions.resolution: Change ANTIGRAVITY §5:131 to "(forbidden for every provider, spec §8 item 8)" to match UNIFIED §I.6.

contradictions.topic: ONBOARDING fallback-branch instruction points to a deleted branch / would fail
contradictions.source_a: ONBOARDING.md:28-30 (copy-paste prompt) — "If those files are not in your checkout, they live on branch docs/threeway-adoption-manuals (PR #19) ... git show origin/docs/threeway-adoption-manuals:docs/protocol/threeway/ONBOARDING.md"
contradictions.source_b: Truth-sources: `git ls-remote --heads origin | grep threeway-adoption` → EMPTY (branch deleted); `git ls-tree origin/main --name-only docs/protocol/threeway/` → all six manuals present on origin/main. The prescribed `git show origin/docs/threeway-adoption-manuals:...` would now FAIL.
contradictions.nature: The onboarding prompt instructs a fallback to a branch that no longer exists; the manuals are merged to main.
contradictions.which_is_right: Truth-sources win. The adoption manuals are on origin/main and the fallback branch is gone. Verified via `git ls-remote --heads origin | grep threeway-adoption` (empty) and `git ls-tree origin/main --name-only docs/protocol/threeway/` (all present).
contradictions.resolution: Delete or rewrite ONBOARDING.md:28-30: the manuals are on main; if missing, "run git fetch origin && git checkout origin/main -- docs/protocol/threeway/". Remove the dead branch/PR#19 reference.

contradictions.topic: Slice-2.5 plan link points at the deferred Jun-19 stub, not the Jun-20 implementation plan
contradictions.source_b: Truth-sources: `ls -la` → the Jun-19 plan is 4732 bytes (the DEFERRED stub); the real implementation plan is docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-cross-provider-seat-topology-slice2.5-legacy-bus-migration.md (172709 bytes). On the Jun-19 stub, `sed -n '70p'` is "The legacy-bus checkers ... stay green" (NOT the dual-write line; dual-write is at the stub's lines 39/74).
contradictions.nature: Both manuals link/anchor the superseded Jun-19 stub; CODEX additionally cites stub:70 for the no-dual-write claim, but that line is a checkers bullet.
contradictions.which_is_right: Truth-sources win. The implementing plan is the Jun-20 172KB file; linking the 4.7KB Jun-19 stub reinforces the stale 'deferred stub' status and the CODEX :70 anchor is wrong (dual-write is at stub:39/74, or better the Jun-20 design §D2:47). Verified via `ls -la` both plans and `sed -n '70p'`/`grep -n 'dual-write'` on the stub.
contradictions.resolution: Repoint UNIFIED §I.5:110 and CODEX §4:103 to the Jun-20 implementation plan (or the Slice-2.5 design spec §D2:47 for the no-dual-write claim); drop the stub:70 anchor.

contradictions.topic: Two-trigger ('release_requested') gating — diagram/spec say BOTH triggers; predicate code has no release_requested clause
contradictions.source_a: ARCHITECTURE-DIAGRAM.md:86 (Bus→Gate edge), :118-120 (read 4), :133 (corrects #3) — "needs BOTH release_requested and release_order"; matches spec §6.3 (line 297) and UNIFIED §I.4 trigger description
contradictions.source_b: Truth-sources: `grep -c release_requested threeway/predicate.py` → 0 — the predicate has NO explicit release_requested gating clause. The coordinator's authority is expressed structurally via the AUTHORITATIVE candidate (predicate.py:51 state.authoritative_candidate, ADR-039/042); release_order is the overseer's separate trigger (predicate.py:137). release_requested IS a real coordinator-signed fact (reducer.py:112 folds it) but is not the gating clause.
contradictions.nature: The diagram (and spec/intent) state two explicit triggers; the current predicate code expresses the coordinator trigger via the authoritative candidate, not a release_requested check — a code/spec-intent gap, not a code/code contradiction.
contradictions.which_is_right: Both are 'right' at different layers: the SPEC/intent two-trigger model holds, but the CODE realizes the coordinator trigger structurally (authoritative_candidate, predicate.py:51) rather than via a release_requested predicate clause (count 0). Verified via `grep -c release_requested threeway/predicate.py` → 0 and `grep -n 'authoritative_candidate' threeway/predicate.py` → 51.
contradictions.resolution: Add a one-line note in ARCHITECTURE-DIAGRAM read 4 (:118) and UNIFIED §I.4: "In v1 the coordinator trigger is enforced structurally via the overseer-bound authoritative candidate (predicate.py:51), not a separate release_requested predicate clause; release_requested remains a folded coordinator fact (reducer.py:112)."

consistency.verdict: The three adoption manuals do NOT agree with the truth-sources or fully with each other. The SEAT->PROVIDER->layer table is the one thing that is consistent everywhere (UNIFIED §I.2, CODEX §2, ANTIGRAVITY §1, ARCHITECTURE-DIAGRAM, spec §4 D2/D5, and threeway/loop.py PAIR_A/PAIR_B all agree); the per-seat 'who signs' authority (overseer brief/cycle_go/release_order, ci ci_result, coordinator candidate) is also consistent with predicate.py and spec §6.3. Beyond those, 18 contradictions were found and verified against HEAD. The dominant, repo-wide problem is STALE BUILD STATUS: README, UNIFIED §I.5, and ARCHITECTURE-DIAGRAM uniformly assert Slice 2.5 is a 'deferred stub' and Slice 3 is 'unbuilt' (with the literal-false claim that co_sign_satisfied returns False for T2/T3), but `ls threeway/` + tier.py + DECISIONS ADR-034/035/043/044/045 + ARCHITECTURE.md §13A prove both slices' substrate is BUILT, merged, hardened, and passing 307 tests — only the live cutover EXECUTION and the Slice-3 scope-(b) fact-emission RUNTIME remain. A second cluster (3 critical + several major) is the coordinator2 harness story: all four touchpoints (CODEX §2/§4/§6, ONBOARDING) claim the Codex harness rejects CODEX_SEAT=coordinator2, but codex_protocol_model.py:104/155-156 and protocol_mailbox.py:17 now bind it as an unpinned coordinator-mode alias. A third cluster is anchor rot: run_gate (gate.py:95→158), store/refstore append, ci_result predicate clause, the 'by seat' comment, the Antigravity signer-check lines, _MAILBOX_SEATS, loop.py ci_result fixture, and the Slice-2.5 spec/plan no-dual-write citation all point at lines that moved. One internal cross-doc inconsistency: ANTIGRAVITY §5 cites the no-dual-write rule as 'spec §8.8' (no such subsection) while sibling UNIFIED §I.6 cites it correctly as 'spec §8 item 8, line 400'. The package itself remains correct and self-consistent and is still wired into nothing live (import grep empty) — the docs are the stale layer, exactly as CLAUDE.md/ARCHITECTURE.md predict. Truth-sources win in every case; the unifying edits are listed per-row.
